children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Armed Services of the Senate;

children.label: C
children.text: the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate;

children.label: D
children.text: the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs of the Senate;

children.label: E
children.text: the Committee on Armed Services of the House of Representatives;

children.label: F
children.text: the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives;

children.label: G
children.text: the Committee on Homeland Security of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: H
children.text: the Committee on Oversight and Reform of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Appropriate industry partner
children.label: 2 Appropriate industry partner
children.text: The term “appropriate industry partner” means a contractor, licensee, or grantee (as defined in section 101(a) of Executive Order No. 12829 ( note; relating to National Industrial Security Program)) that is participating in the National Industrial Security Program established by such Executive order.50 U.S.C. 3161

children.heading: Continuous vetting
children.label: 3 Continuous vetting
children.text: The term “continuous vetting” has the meaning given such term in Executive Order No. 13467 ( note; relating to reforming processes for determining suitability for government employment, fitness for contractor employees, and eligibility for access to classified national security information).50 U.S.C. 3161

children.heading: Council
children.label: 4 Council
children.text: The term “Council” means the Security, Suitability, and Credentialing Performance Accountability Council established pursuant to such Executive order, or any successor entity.

children.heading: Reciprocity
children.label: 5 Reciprocity
children.text: The term “reciprocity” means reciprocal recognition by Federal departments and agencies of eligibility for access to classified information.

children.heading: Security Executive Agent
children.label: 6 Security Executive Agent
children.text: The term “Security Executive Agent” means the officer serving as the Security Executive Agent pursuant to .section 3162a of this title

children.heading: Suitability and Credentialing Executive Agent
children.label: 7 Suitability and Credentialing Executive Agent
children.text: The term “Suitability and Credentialing Executive Agent” means the Director of the Office of Personnel Management acting as the Suitability and Credentialing Executive Agent in accordance with Executive Order No. 13467 ( note; relating to reforming processes related to suitability for Government employment, fitness for contractor employees, and eligibility for access to classified national security information), or any successor entity.50 U.S.C. 3161
